scenario: Aurora airline routes 9000 events per day through a booking process in Camunda 8.8. An escalation throw event must raise an escalationCode that depends on the fare class detected at runtime so that different catch handlers can be triggered downstream.
question: Which value forms does Camunda 8.8 accept for the escalationCode on an escalation throw event?

options.text: A static escalationCode value only; FEEL expressions cannot be configured for the escalationCode on the throw event in this version
A FEEL expression only; static escalationCode values are accepted only on the escalation catch event reference of the enclosing scope
A static value or a FEEL expression; the expression is evaluated when the throw event is reached and the result is used to throw the escalation
A static value combined with a FEEL expression that returns a fallback escalationCode if the static value cannot be resolved at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8.8 allows the escalationCode on a throw event to be defined as a FEEL expression in addition to a static value.

b.text: Incorrect. Static values are valid on escalation throw events as well as on catch events; FEEL expressions are not the only accepted form on the throw side.

c.text: Correct. An escalation throw event accepts the escalationCode as either a static value or a FEEL expression; the expression is evaluated when the event is reached and the result is used to throw the escalation.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no fallback combination of a static value with a FEEL expression on the throw event; the escalationCode is configured as one form.

explanation: An escalation throw event in Camunda 8.8 accepts the escalationCode either as a static value or as a FEEL expression. When configured as an expression, the engine evaluates it once the event is reached and uses the result to throw the escalation, while catch events require a static value.
